title: Scorzoneroides × jouffroyi
intro: Scorzoneroides × jouffroyi, also known as Jouffroy's scorpion's tail, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its unique, spiny flower heads. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving digestive issues and supporting immune function. Bioactive constituents of Scorzoneroides × jouffroyi include sesquiterpene lactones, such as scorpionin, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Scorzoneroides × jouffroyi is a hybrid species within the genus Scorzoneroides, which belongs to the family Compositae (also known as Asteraceae). The family Compositae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Scorzoneroides is further divided into several species, with Scorzoneroides × jouffroyi being a unique hybrid. This classification places Scorzoneroides × jouffroyi within the broader context of flowering plants. Its specific classification is likely to be subject to ongoing research and refinement.
